name: Cyn's Focaccia
recipeIngredient: 3 cups Bread or all purpose flour
1/4 cup Extra virgin olive oil plus
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift flour and salt into a large bowl.
2. Dissolve yeast and sugar in warm milk in a small bowl (or a Pyrex measuring cup used to warm the milk). Let stand about 10 minutes until frothy.
3. Stir in the olive oil. Gradually beat the yeast mixture into the flour mixture using a wooden spoon to give a soft, but not sticky, dough.
4. Knead on a lightly floured surface for 5 minutes until smooth and elastic.
5. Place the dough in an oiled medium-size bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm place about 40 minutes until doubled in size.
6. Turn out onto a lightly floured surface and knead 5 more minutes.
7. Oil a baking sheet.
8. Roll out the dough to a large circle (or squares) that is about 1/2 inch thick and transfer to the sheet.
9. Brush the dough with olive oil, sprinkle with rosemary and sea salt (and any other toppings you like), and lightly press into the dough.
10. Make deep indentations over the surface using your finger.
11. Let rise for another 25 minutes.
12. Preheat the oven to 425°F.
13. Bake for 15-20 minutes or until golden.
14. Brush with olive oil again and serve warm.
